introSection: Galaxy Spin is a steel spinning wild mouse roller coaster at Fun Spot America in Kissimmee, Florida, USA. It was previously located at Cypress Gardens Adventure Park.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Galaxy Spin opened at Cypress Gardens Adventure Park on August 17, 2005. It operated until 2008, when the park closed due to bankruptcy. It reopened at Fun Spot America as Powertrip Coaster from 2011 to 2012. In 2013, it was changed to Rockstar Coaster. Then in 2019, it was changed back to Galaxy Spin.

The ride was temporarily closed following an accident on August 3, 2023. It reopened later that same year.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: A 6-year-old boy was severely injured after apparently falling from the ride on August 3, 2023. The coaster was closed indefinitely after the incident but reopened before the end of September.

fullText:
Galaxy Spin is a steel spinning wild mouse roller coaster at Fun Spot America in Kissimmee, Florida, USA. It was previously located at Cypress Gardens Adventure Park.

Galaxy Spin opened at Cypress Gardens Adventure Park on August 17, 2005. It operated until 2008, when the park closed due to bankruptcy. It reopened at Fun Spot America as Powertrip Coaster from 2011 to 2012. In 2013, it was changed to Rockstar Coaster. Then in 2019, it was changed back to Galaxy Spin.

The ride was temporarily closed following an accident on August 3, 2023. It reopened later that same year.





A 6-year-old boy was severely injured after apparently falling from the ride on August 3, 2023. The coaster was closed indefinitely after the incident but reopened before the end of September.
